Dispatches from the Weave

Season One — 12 chapters, now written Mira Dawnhammer is recruiting Forgeborn First Worldwound stirs: The Long Stillness Guilds of up to 10 now forming Personal Trials live for solo warriors

The Weave

Every Rep Rebuilds the World

Aethon is dying. The Weave — the force that binds it together — has always been sustained by physical effort, and for too long, no one has trained hard enough to hold it. Thirty years ago, the scholar-warrior Mira Dawnhammer rediscovered that truth and founded the Forgeborn Order in the mountain city of Kaelthas. She's been fighting the Void's advance ever since — "Train, and it becomes clear," she says — and now she's recruiting you.

Log a real workout and it's verified automatically — no workout, no XP. Every session earns experience, raises your stats, and pushes forward a serialised story told one chapter at a time. Skip a week, and Aethon feels it. Aldric Vesper, your mentor bound to the Weave itself, always notices.

Choose Your Path

Three Classes. One Weave.

Your class isn't cosmetic — it's the real training style that powers it. Pick the one that matches how you already move, or the one you want to become.

STRENGTH

Ironguard

Strength given form. The wall Aethon puts between itself and the dark. Slow to tire, impossible to move.

WeightsHIITBodyweight

AGILITY · STAMINA

Wayfarer

Speed and endurance made manifest — the fastest thread in the Weave. Aethon's messages, and its rescues, move through you.

RunningCyclingEndurance

WISDOM

Stillmind

Focus honed into power. The rarest gift in Aethon, because it's earned through discipline — never through force.

YogaFlexibilityMobility

How it Works

From the Gym Floor to the Story

I

Create your Forgeborn

Choose Ironguard, Wayfarer, or Stillmind. Your class shapes your stats, your abilities, and the chapters written for you.

II

Train in the real world

Log a session or sync Apple Health. It's verified automatically — no workout, no XP. That's the whole rule.

III

Level up, move the story

Every session earns XP, raises your stats, and advances Season One — 12 chapters that only move when you do.

IV

Raid together, or alone

Join a guild and take down weekly Raid Bosses as a team — or run Personal Trials solo with Sable and Renn at your side.

Guild Raids

No One Reps Alone

Form a guild of up to ten and take on a Worldwound together — a tear in the Weave that only closes through real, collective training. Start with The Long Stillness, a tutorial-tier wound almost any new guild can close on its first attempt, and climb through The Hollow Choir and The Iron Tide toward the true test: The Mirrorfall — an endgame Worldwound whose Decay Bar is split across all four stats. Guilds who only run cardio, or only lift, don't close it. Guilds who train across every class do.

Training solo? Personal Trials mirror the same ladder — from four sessions a week for The Long Stillness up to five sessions across multiple training types for The Mirrorfall — guided by your companions Sable and Renn, who show up whether or not anyone else does.

Your Mentor

Aldric Vesper Is Watching

Aldric Vesper is the scholar assigned to guide you — quiet, precise, and prone to knowing more than he lets on. He's studied the Stillening for decades, and he's built on the same intelligence that answers when you open the app: Claude, reading your training and shaping your plan around it.

"The Weave responds to real effort, not intention."

— Aldric Vesper, Scholar & AI Mentor

The Gates Are Opening

The first 500 recruits to join the waitlist unlock Founding Warrior status at launch — before Forgeborn opens to the rest of Aethon.
